errors.notFoundBody: The page or artwork you requested does not exist or may have been moved.

meta.title: Ferhat Çubukçu — Painter
meta.description: Contemporary painting — Fibonacci, golden ratio, and exhibition work.

home.phiMeasure: Golden ratio — 1.61803… · a quiet measure in every composition
home.statementLead: Spirals drawn from Fibonacci’s approach to the golden ratio, woven through figures and compositions — often at 80×100 cm and beyond.
home.featuredSubtitle: Browse the full gallery for every published piece.
home.newsTeaserSubtitle: Exhibitions, social posts, and updates from the studio.
home.pressTeaserSubtitle: Quotes and coverage — add entries in Site settings.

gallery.title: Gallery
gallery.seoDescription: Gallery of paintings by Ferhat Çubukçu — Fibonacci, golden ratio, and figurative composition.
gallery.empty: Works will appear here once you add them in the admin panel.
gallery.chronologySortNote: Works are grouped by year. Use grid view to change sort order.
gallery.browseHint: Tap or click any painting to open its detail page — dimensions, description, and inquiry.
gallery.paintingZoomHint: Ctrl or ⌘ + scroll to zoom. Drag to pan when zoomed. Double-click toggles zoom.
gallery.paintingZoomSwipeHint: Swipe left or right for the next or previous painting.

press.title: Press
press.intro: Exhibitions, coverage, social posts, and studio notes — one feed.
press.seoDescription: Press and studio updates from painter Ferhat Çubukçu.

publicAdmin.galleryFavoriteHint: Use the gold box under each painting to mark Ferhat's selection.
publicAdmin.confirmDeleteArtwork: Remove this painting from the site? This cannot be undone.
publicAdmin.confirmDeleteNews: Remove this update from the site? This cannot be undone.
publicAdmin.favoriteHint: Gold frame and badge on the site; listed first in the gallery.

about.title: About
about.seoDescription: Biography and studio practice — Fibonacci and golden ratio in composition, exhibitions in Türkiye.

contact.title: Contact
contact.seoDescription: Contact Ferhat Çubukçu for commissions, exhibitions, and studio visits.
contact.reachOut: For commissions, exhibitions, or studio visits, please write.
contact.trustShipping: Shipping, VAT, and customs depend on destination and current regulations — details are confirmed in writing before any sale.
contact.trustOrdering: Availability and pricing on this site are for reference; a short email thread usually clarifies framing, delivery, and payment.

studioPage.title: Studio & practice
studioPage.seoDescription: Materials, process, and how the work is made — Ferhat Çubukçu.
studioPage.intro: A concise look at how paintings move from idea to finished canvas.
studioPage.b1Body: Works are built on canvas and panel in a range of media — often at 80×100 cm and larger — layered so light and texture stay alive in the surface.
studioPage.b2Body: Fibonacci’s approach to the golden ratio informs spacing and rhythm: spirals and intervals appear in overall compositions and between forms, never as a rigid grid.
studioPage.b3Body: Figures are treated as part of the same compositional field as architecture and space — weight, gesture, and negative space are weighed together.
studioPage.b4Body: Palette choices follow the subject and the time of day implied in the scene; warm earths and cooled neutrals keep large passages readable from a distance.
studioPage.b5Body: Close views and edge treatment matter: detail photographs on this site show texture, frame, and how paint sits on the weave.
studioPage.b6Body: For availability, commissions, or exhibition proposals, use Contact — a short exchange usually clarifies intent, timeline, and delivery.

curator.seoDescription: Printable list of published works for curators and galleries.

socialPage.title: Social Media
socialPage.intro: Recent posts from the studio on Instagram — tap an image to open it there.
socialPage.seoDescription: Social feed — studio posts, process, and exhibitions from painter Ferhat Çubukçu.
socialPage.emptyPosts: No posts were returned for this account yet.
socialPage.configureP1: To show every post here, this site uses Meta’s Instagram Graph API. The account must be a professional Instagram profile (Business or Creator) linked to a Facebook Page — personal accounts cannot be queried by the API.
socialPage.configureP2: Create a long-lived access token in the Meta developer tools, then set INSTAGRAM_USER_ID (the Instagram business account ID) and INSTAGRAM_ACCESS_TOKEN on the server (for example in Vercel environment variables). Redeploy after saving.
socialPage.configureP3: See .env.example in the repository for variable names. Until then, use the button above to visit the profile on Instagram.
socialPage.loadErrorHint: The token may have expired or lost permissions. Generate a new long-lived token in Meta for Developers and update INSTAGRAM_ACCESS_TOKEN, then redeploy.

footer.rights: © Ferhat Çubukçu. All works reserved unless noted.

admin.newsIntro: Bilingual posts with optional image and external link (Instagram, press).
admin.detailImagesHint: Optional close-ups, frame, or texture. Choose many files at once (Shift or Ctrl/Cmd + click) — up to 24 per painting.
admin.detailImagesMax: This painting already has 24 detail photos. Remove some before adding more.
admin.exhibitionHint: Optional — e.g. group show title and city.
admin.soldHint: Sold works remain in the public gallery with a SOLD label on the image.
admin.favoriteHint: Highlighted on the site with a gold frame and special badge; favorites appear first in the gallery.
admin.priceHint: Optional. Free text — e.g. €4,500, TL equivalent, or “On request”. Shown on gallery and painting pages; leave blank to hide.
admin.seriesHint: Optional. Same slug on multiple paintings groups them for gallery filters (e.g. portraits-2024).
admin.pressQuotesHint: Short quotes with attribution. Optional logo/image URL and link to the source.
admin.galleryImageFitLabel: Fit full painting in frame (show entire image without cropping)
admin.galleryImageFitHint: Off: images fill the card and may crop edges (current look). On: the whole painting is visible inside the frame.
